ogTitle: Higher Macdonald vs Highfields: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Higher Macdonald and Highfields.
heading: Higher Macdonald vs Highfields — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Higher Macdonald, New South Wales and Highfields, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Highfields has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Higher Macdonald (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Higher Macdonald or Highfields safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Higher Macdonald has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Highfields scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Higher Macdonald scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Highfields scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Higher Macdonald or Highfields?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Highfields has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
